zh.lifeSummary: 當William Babington於大約1369年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾易亞斯特 布利其福德時，他的父親John de Babington IV37歲，母親Benidicta de La Warde33歲。他於1396年與Margery Martell在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾爾維爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1423年被列為chief justice of the king's bench。他去世於1455年在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾爾維爾享年87歲，埋葬在英國諾廷加姆爾爾維爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Babington于大约1369年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾易亞斯特 布利其福德时，他的父亲John de Babington IV37岁，母亲Benidicta de La Warde33岁。他于1396年与Margery Martell在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾爾維爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1423年被列为chief justice of the king's bench。他去世于1455年在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾爾維爾享年87岁，埋葬在英国諾廷加姆爾爾維爾。

nameMeanings.name: Babington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name for someone from Babington in Somerset or Great or Little Bavington in Northumberland, which is recorded as Babington until the early 18th century. Both are named with the Old English personal name Babba (see Babb ) + the connective particle -ing- ‘associated with, named after’ + tūn ‘settlement’. The name may also be derived from Bebington in Cheshire (see 2 below) or Bevington in Warwickshire (see Bevington , which has sometimes been altered in spelling by association with Babington).

English (Cheshire and Lancashire): variant of Bebbington, a habitational name from Bebington in Cheshire, from the Old English personal name Bebba + the connective particle -ing- ‘associated with, named after’ + tūn ‘settlement’. The alteration to Bavington and Bevington shows a common development of -b- to -v- in placenames and surnames.

variants.name: Babb
meanings: English (Devon, Somerset, and Cornwall): from the Middle English personal name Babb(e), usually no doubt a pet form of the female personal name Barbara (see Barbara ), but possibly in some cases a survival of the Old English personal name Babba, which is found in several placenames, including Babbacombe in Devon and Babington in Somerset.
English (Devon, Somerset, and Cornwall): from Middle English babe ‘infant, young child’, applied as a nickname.
Americanized form of German   Bobb   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Bevington
meanings: (southwest Midlands): habitational name from either of two places, in Warwickshire and Gloucestershire, named Bevington, from the Old English personal name Bēofa + Old English -ing- implying association + tūn ‘settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When William Babington was born about 1369, in East Bridgford, Nottinghamshire, England, his father, John de Babington IV, was 37 and his mother, Benidicta de La Warde, was 33. He married Margery Martell in 1396, in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. In 1423, at the age of 55, his occupation is listed as chief justice of the king's bench. He died in 1455, in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 87, and was buried in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Babington est né environ 1369, à East Bridgford, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Babington IV, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Benidicta de La Warde, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Margery Martell en 1396, à Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. En 1423, à l’âge de 55 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est chief justice of the king's bench. Il est décédé en 1455, à Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 87 ans, et a été enterré à Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Babington um 1369 in East Bridgford, Nottinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Babington IV 37 und seine Mutter Benidicta de La Warde 33. Er hat 1396 in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Margery Martell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1423, als er 55 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als chief justice of the king's bench aufgeführt. Er starb 1455 im Alter von 87 Jahren in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Babington è nato il circa 1369 nel East Bridgford, Nottinghamshire, England, il suo padre, John de Babington IV, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Benidicta de La Warde, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Margery Martell nel 1396, nel Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Nel 1423, all'età di 55 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come chief justice of the king's bench. Morì nel 1455, nel Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 87 anni, e fu sepolto a Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: William Babingtonが約1369年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアエアスト ブリドグフォードで生まれた時，父John de Babington IVは37歳，母Benidicta de La Wardeは33歳であった。1396年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアチルウエルでMargery Martellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。1423年，55歳の時，職業はchief justice of the king's benchと記録されている。彼は1455年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアチルウエルで87歳で死去し，イギリスノッティンガムシュアイアチルウエルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Babington(이)가 대략 1369년 영국 노틴그함실르 이스트 브리지퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Babington IV(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Benidicta de La Warde(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1396년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 치웰에서 Margery Martell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1423년, 55세의 나이로 그의 직업은 chief justice of the king's bench이었다. 그는 1455년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 치웰에서 87세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노틴그함실르 치웰에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Babington nasceu aproximadamente 1369, em East Bridgford, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Babington IV, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Benidicta de La Warde, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery Martell em 1396, em Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Em 1423, com 55 anos, sua profissão aparece como chief justice of the king's bench. Ele faleceu em 1455, em Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 87 anos, e foi sepultado em Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Babington родился около 1369 г. в местности эаст бриджфорд, ноттингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Babington IV было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Benidicta de La Warde, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Martell в 1396 г. в местности чилвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. В 1423 году, в возрасте 55 лет,  он работал по профессии chief justice of the king's bench. Он умер в 1455 г. в местности чилвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 87 лет и был похоронен в местности чилвелл, ноттингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Babington nació aproximadamente 1369, en East Bridgford, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Babington IV, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Benidicta de La Warde, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Margery Martell en 1396, en Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. En 1423, a la edad de 55 años, su ocupación registrada era chief justice of the king's bench. Murió en 1455, en Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 87 años, y fue sepultado en Chilwell, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra.
